evals.prompt: Program a liquid transfer on our Hamilton STAR using PyLabRobot: pick up tips, transfer 100 uL from a source 96-well plate to a destination plate, and drop tips.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-pylabrobot. Uses PyLabRobot's LiquidHandler with the STAR backend and a STARLetDeck, assigns resources, and runs the async protocol (await lh.setup(), pick_up_tips, transfer/aspirate+dispense, drop_tips).

assertions.value: Uses the STAR backend with a deck and awaits the async liquid-handling calls.

evals.prompt: I need one protocol that runs liquid handling and then reads absorbance on a BMG CLARIOstar plate reader, all from the same Python script.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-pylabrobot. Integrates analytical equipment: uses a PlateReader with the CLARIOstarBackend (await pr.setup, read_absorbance) alongside the LiquidHandler in one script, leveraging PyLabRobot's unified cross-device interface.

assertions.value: Drives a PlateReader (CLARIOstarBackend, read_absorbance) and a LiquidHandler from the same async script.

evals.prompt: Before I run anything on the real robot I want to simulate my PyLabRobot protocol and watch the deck state. How do I do that?
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-pylabrobot. Routes to visualization/simulation: swap in the LiquidHandlerChatterboxBackend and attach a Visualizer (Visualizer(resource=lh); await vis.setup()) to watch tip presence and liquid volumes without physical hardware.

assertions.value: Uses a no-hardware simulation backend (LiquidHandlerChatterboxBackend) and starts the Visualizer to inspect deck state before running on hardware.

evals.prompt: Coordinate a workflow that uses a Hamilton heater-shaker and an Inheco incubator alongside the liquid handler, all controlled from one PyLabRobot script.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-pylabrobot. Routes to material handling: controls the heater-shaker and incubator (temperature/shaking) together with the liquid handler through PyLabRobot's unified multi-device interface and backend abstraction.

assertions.value: Coordinates multiple device types (heater-shaker, incubator, liquid handler) through PyLabRobot's unified interface.

evals.prompt: Write me a standard Opentrons OT-2 protocol with the official Protocol API v2 to do a PCR setup on the thermocycler module.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-opentrons. The user explicitly wants an Opentrons-only protocol using the official Opentrons Protocol API v2, which the dedicated Opentrons skill handles more simply than PyLabRobot's vendor-agnostic layer.
